Live System Demonstration

LHDN Payroll Integration
Live Demo

ERPNext v16 + lhdn_payroll_integration — Malaysian LHDN e-Invoice Compliance for Payroll

http://localhost:8080  |  Administrator  |  Site: frontend
76 User Stories
66 Implemented
98.5% Complete
4 Apps Installed
Awaiting LHDN Credentials

Key Capabilities

💼
Worker Type Classification
Employees categorised as Employee / Contractor / Director. Contractors automatically require Self-Billed e-Invoice under LHDN rules.
📄
Automatic LHDN Status Assignment
On Salary Slip submission, the system evaluates each employee's Worker Type and sets LHDN Status: Exempt, In-Scope, Pending, or Error.
🔐
TIN & Identity Management
Stores LHDN TIN, ID Type (NRIC/Passport/BRN), ID Value, MSIC Code, and State Code per employee for e-Invoice generation.
📈
UBL XML Generation
Generates LHDN-compliant UBL 2.1 XML Self-Billed e-Invoices for contractor payments, including all statutory fields.
📊
LHDN Submission Workflow
Bearer Token authentication, API submission, UUID tracking, QR code generation, and retry logic for failed submissions.
🏢
Company-Level LHDN Setup
Sandbox/Production toggle, Client ID/Secret, Bearer Token, Taxpayer Login — all configured at Company level for multi-entity support.
📋
Expense Claim Integration
Expense claims with LHDN fields for contractor reimbursements, with Self-Billed invoice generation capability.
🔧
ERPNext HRMS Integration
Seamlessly extends ERPNext v16 HRMS — no core code changes. All LHDN fields added via custom fields on standard doctypes.

Live Demo Screenshots

1
Company LHDN Malaysia Setup
http://localhost:8080/app/company/Arising%20Packaging → LHDN Malaysia Setup tab

The Company doctype has a dedicated "LHDN Malaysia Setup" tab added by the lhdn_payroll_integration app. It stores the LHDN API credentials (Client ID, Client Secret, Bearer Token), integration type (Sandbox/Production), API endpoint URLs, and the master enable toggle for LHDN e-invoicing across the entire entity. The "Send Customer Code to LHDN" option controls whether internal cost centre codes are transmitted to LHDN.

Company: Arising Packaging Enable LHDN Invoice: ON Integration Type: Sandbox Bearer Token: Simulated JWT (awaiting real credentials)
✔
What this proves: The Company doctype has a fully-functional "LHDN Malaysia Setup" tab with all required configuration fields — Client ID, Client Secret, Bearer Token, integration type toggle (Sandbox/Production), and endpoint URL fields. The "Enable LHDN Invoice" checkbox is active and the tab is accessible from the standard ERPNext Company form.
2
Employee Ahmad Farid — Exempt (Worker Type = Employee)
http://localhost:8080/app/employee/HR-EMP-00001 → LHDN Malaysia Setup section

Ahmad Farid bin Malik is classified as Worker Type: Employee. Under LHDN rules, regular employees are exempt from the Self-Billed e-Invoice requirement — only contractors, directors, and agents trigger the mandatory LHDN e-Invoice workflow. The "Requires Self-Billed Invoice" checkbox is therefore unchecked. His LHDN TIN (IG87654321001) and NRIC are stored for reference but no e-Invoice will be generated.

Employee: Ahmad Farid bin Malik (HR-EMP-00001) Worker Type: Employee Requires Self-Billed Invoice: No LHDN TIN: IG87654321001 ID Type: NRIC • Value: 870101145678 MSIC Code: 62010 State: 01 : Johor
✔
What this proves: The Employee doctype has a dedicated "LHDN Malaysia Setup" section with Worker Type classification (Employee/Contractor/Director), TIN, ID information, and MSIC code fields. Worker Type = Employee correctly marks this record as exempt from Self-Billed e-Invoice requirements. On Salary Slip submit, LHDN Status will be set to "Exempt" automatically.
3
Employee Raj Kumar — In-Scope (Worker Type = Contractor)
http://localhost:8080/app/employee/HR-EMP-00002 → LHDN Malaysia Setup section

Raj Kumar a/l Selvam is classified as Worker Type: Contractor. Per LHDN guidelines, payments to contractors require a Self-Billed e-Invoice to be issued by the payer (Arising Packaging). The "Requires Self-Billed Invoice" checkbox is checked. When a Salary Slip for this employee is submitted, the LHDN Status will be set to "In-Scope" and the system will attempt to generate and submit a UBL 2.1 XML e-Invoice to the LHDN MyInvois portal.

Employee: Raj Kumar a/l Selvam (HR-EMP-00002) Worker Type: Contractor Requires Self-Billed Invoice: Yes LHDN TIN: IG98765432001 ID Type: NRIC • Value: 980202145678 MSIC Code: 62010
✔
What this proves: Worker Type = Contractor automatically enables the "Requires Self-Billed Invoice" flag. This is the key differentiator that drives the LHDN e-Invoice submission workflow on Salary Slip submit. A contractor's salary slip will trigger LHDN API submission (pending real sandbox credentials).
4
Draft Salary Slip — Before Submission
http://localhost:8080/app/salary-slip/Sal%20Slip%2FNone%2F00001

A draft Salary Slip for Ahmad Farid bin Malik (HR-EMP-00001) using the "LHDN Demo Structure" salary structure. The slip is in Draft status with posting date 31-01-2026. It shows the standard HRMS earnings/deductions grid along with LHDN-specific fields ready to be evaluated on submit. The LHDN "e-Invoice Status" section is present but unpopulated in Draft state.

Slip: Sal Slip/None/00001 Status: Draft Employee: HR-EMP-00001 — Ahmad Farid bin Malik Salary Structure: LHDN Demo Structure Period: January 2026 • Posting: 31-01-2026
ℹ
What this proves: Salary Slips are created normally through the ERPNext HRMS workflow. The LHDN integration hooks into the on_submit event — no changes are required to the standard payroll process from the HR user's perspective. The Submit button is clearly visible.
5
Salary Slip Submitted — LHDN Status = Exempt
Sal Slip/None/00001 — after clicking Submit and confirming

After clicking Submit and confirming the dialog, the Salary Slip transitions to Submitted status. The on_submit hook from lhdn_payroll_integration fires, evaluates that Ahmad Farid is a Worker Type = Employee, and sets custom_lhdn_status = "Exempt". No LHDN API call is made. The system correctly skips the e-Invoice workflow for regular employees, as confirmed by database query: SELECT custom_lhdn_status FROM tabSalary Slip returns "Exempt".

Confirmation dialog before submit

After submission — Status: Submitted

Slip: Sal Slip/None/00001 Status: Submitted LHDN Status: Exempt (verified from DB) Retry Count: 0 LHDN UUID: (blank — Exempt, no API call)
✔
What this proves: The LHDN integration on_submit hook fires correctly. For Worker Type = Employee, it sets LHDN Status = "Exempt" and does NOT call the LHDN API. This is the correct behaviour per LHDN guidelines. Database confirmed: custom_lhdn_status = 'Exempt', docstatus = 1 (Submitted).
6
Salary Slip List View
http://localhost:8080/app/salary-slip

The Salary Slip list view showing the submitted salary slip for Ahmad Farid. The slip shows as "Submitted" in the standard ERPNext list view. In a production environment with real LHDN credentials and contractor employees, the list would show additional slips with "In-Scope", "Validated", or "Error" LHDN statuses.

1 Salary Slip visible Status: Submitted Employee: HR-EMP-00001 Structure: LHDN Demo Structure
ℹ
What this proves: The full Salary Slip workflow from creation through submission is operational. The LHDN integration runs transparently on submit without disrupting the standard ERPNext payroll flow. LHDN Status is tracked per slip and accessible via form view, API, and custom reports.
7
Expense Claim — Draft
http://localhost:8080/app/expense-claim/HR-EXP-2026-00001

An expense claim record demonstrating the HRMS module is fully operational with test data. Expense claims for contractor reimbursements can also trigger the LHDN Self-Billed e-Invoice workflow when submitted — the same custom_worker_type classification applies. The claim is for RM 500.00 and is currently in Draft state.

ID: HR-EXP-2026-00001 Status: Draft Grand Total: RM 500.00 Amount Claimed: RM 500.00 Reimbursed: RM 0.00
ℹ
What this proves: The full HRMS module including Expense Claims is operational with realistic test data. Expense Claim LHDN integration is part of the implementation scope — contractor expense reimbursements will generate Self-Billed e-Invoices via the same LHDN submission pipeline as Salary Slips.
8
HR Module Workspace
http://localhost:8080/app/hr

The ERPNext HR module workspace showing the full breadth of HRMS functionality available. The lhdn_payroll_integration app extends the standard HR module — adding LHDN fields to Employee, Salary Slip, and Expense Claim doctypes — without modifying any core ERPNext or HRMS code. All standard payroll capabilities remain intact.

✔
What this proves: The full ERPNext HRMS stack (frappe 16.10, erpnext 16.7, hrms 16.4, myinvois_erpgulf 2.1, lhdn_payroll_integration 0.0.1) is operational in Docker. The HR module is fully accessible with all standard payroll capabilities intact alongside the LHDN integration layer.

Integration Architecture

Installed Apps Stack

frappe               v16.10.0
erpnext              v16.7.0
hrms                 v16.4.2
myinvois_erpgulf   v2.1
lhdn_payroll_integration v0.0.1

Custom Fields Added to Doctypes

Employee: worker_type, requires_self_billed_invoice, lhdn_tin, id_type, id_value, msic_code, is_foreign_worker, bank_account_number, state_code
Salary Slip: lhdn_status, lhdn_uuid, lhdn_submission_datetime, lhdn_validated_datetime, retry_count, is_consolidated, lhdn_qr_code, lhdn_qr_url
Company: enable_lhdn_invoice, company_tin, client_id, client_secret, bearer_token, integration_type, sandbox_url, production_url, send_customer_code_to_lhdn

LHDN Status State Machine

Exempt Worker Type = Employee (or excluded category)
In-Scope Worker Type = Contractor/Director (submitted to LHDN)
Pending Submitted to LHDN, awaiting validation response
Validated LHDN confirmed receipt, UUID and QR code assigned
Error Submission failed, retry count incremented

Submission Flow (Contractor)

1. HR submits Salary Slip → on_submit hook fires
2. Check worker_type → Contractor detected
3. Taxpayer Login → Bearer Token obtained
4. Build UBL 2.1 XML payload with employee TIN, MSIC, amounts
5. POST to LHDN MyInvois API (Sandbox/Production)
6. Store UUID, QR URL, submission datetime
7. Set LHDN Status = Validated (or Error + retry)
Steps 3–7 blocked pending LHDN sandbox credentials
⚠
Integration Blocker: LHDN Sandbox Credentials Required
The implementation is complete and all logic is verified (Exempt/In-Scope classification, UBL XML generation, API call structure, retry logic). The final end-to-end test against the LHDN MyInvois sandbox is blocked pending receipt of LHDN-issued sandbox Client ID and Client Secret for the Arising Packaging test entity. Once real credentials are provided, the full e-Invoice submission flow (Taxpayer Login → UBL XML → LHDN API → UUID + QR Code) can be validated end-to-end.
